expectations.text: report_has_structured_format: Report contains the Phase 5 format with a 'Coherence Check Report' header and at least two of: 'Auto-Fixed', 'Needs Manual Review', 'Verified Clean', 'Coverage' sections
expectations.evidence: Report has 'Coherence Check Report' header, 'Phase 1 — Change Discovery', 'Auto-Fixed' (None), 'Needs Manual Review' (5 items), 'Verified Clean' section, 'Coverage' section with 20+ files listed

expectations.text: searches_worker_files: Report searches worker files (workers/) for references to the Asset model's url or endpoint_url field — not just describes that it should check workers, but actually reports findings
expectations.evidence: Coverage section lists 'workers/business_logic/tools/*.py — 9 files', 'workers/info_gathering/tools/*.py — 14+ files', and 5 other worker paths. MISMATCH-4 shows 25+ specific worker file references with line numbers for asset_type=="url" patterns.

expectations.text: checks_schema_sql: Report explicitly checks shared/schema.sql for the Asset table's url/endpoint_url column
expectations.evidence: MISMATCH-1 explicitly compares ORM columns against schema.sql DDL and finds 3 missing columns (scope_classification, associated_with_id, association_method). MISMATCH-2 finds job_state.last_completed_stage missing from schema.sql.

expectations.text: checks_database_py: Report explicitly checks shared/lib_webbh/database.py Asset ORM model for field name
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 explicitly reads 'shared/lib_webbh/database.py, class Asset, lines 233-266' and confirms Asset ORM model columns. MISMATCH-1 cites specific ORM column definitions.

expectations.text: checks_dashboard_types: Report checks dashboard TypeScript types (dashboard/src/ or shared/interfaces.ts) for references to the url or endpoint_url field on Asset
expectations.evidence: MISMATCH-3 cites 'shared/interfaces.ts:54-60 defines Asset with only id, target_id, asset_type, asset_value, source_tool — missing tech, scope_classification, associated_with_id, association_method'. Coverage lists dashboard/src/app/campaign/assets/page.tsx and dashboard/src/types/schema.ts.

expectations.text: coverage_section_lists_files: The Coverage section (or equivalent) names at least 5 specific file paths that were actually inspected
expectations.evidence: Coverage section lists 20+ specific paths including shared/lib_webbh/database.py, shared/schema.sql, shared/interfaces.ts, dashboard/src/types/schema.ts, dashboard/src/lib/api.ts, 5 dashboard pages, orchestrator/main.py, and multiple worker directories

claims.claim: schema.sql assets table is missing 3 ORM columns (scope_classification, associated_with_id, association_method)
claims.evidence: MISMATCH-1 cites database.py lines 233-266 and schema.sql lines 57-72 with column-by-column comparison. Workers that write these fields are also cited (base_tool.py:111, main.py:94, pipeline.py:375-377).

claims.claim: schema.sql job_state table is missing last_completed_stage column
claims.evidence: MISMATCH-2 cites database.py:408 (ORM), schema.sql:200-221 (DDL, column absent), dashboard/src/types/schema.ts:145 (TypeScript references it)

suggestions.reason: An assertion checking whether the agent found REAL mismatches beyond the hypothetical scenario would better discriminate skill quality. The with-skill agent found 4 genuine issues; without-skill found 0. The current assertions only check process (did it look?), not outcome (did it find real problems?)

eval_feedback.overall: Strong result. The skill's systematic ORM-vs-DDL comparison check is clearly what drove the real finding discovery.
